extra.message: Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into fs/promises.readdir, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to list arbitrary directories.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into fs/promises.readFile, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into fs/promises.readFile, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into exec, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into prepare, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
Hardcoded value is used as a cipher key (in crypto.default.createHmac). Generate the value with a cryptographically strong random number generator and do not hardcode it in source code.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into fetch, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into a script 'src' attribute, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into a script 'src' attribute, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into a script 'src' attribute, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into a script 'src' attribute, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in passwordHash.
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in passwordHash.

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into __html, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).

Unsanitized input from a React useState value flows into a React dynamic 'href' attribute, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).

Unsanitized input from data from a remote resource flows into fs/promises.readFile, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.

Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into fs.writeFileSync, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to write to arbitrary files.
crypto.default.createHash hash (used in crypto.default.createHash) is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
crypto.default.createHash hash (used in crypto.default.createHash) is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
